verify.attestation_lifecycle: issue -> verify -> revoke. verify_attestation recomputes attestation_id from body (tamper detection: id_ok) + checks revocation. attestation_ledger.py = hash-chain issue/revoke events (verdict-ledger discipline, record_hash time-independent, tamper-evident). CLI verify-attestation / revoke-attestation; attest/audit gain --att-ledger. Smoke: issue idx0 -> verify valid -> revoke -> verify invalid (reason=revoked, id_ok still true), exit 1.
verify.closed_audit_loop: attestra_audit.run_audit + `attestra audit <dir>`: batch ingest -> route (override/pack-field/filename) -> verdict -> attest non-breach -> single hash-chain ledger -> verify. Smoke: 12 packets across 4 packs -> 4 valid/4 thin/4 breach, 8 attestations, chain valid 12 records, exit 1 on breach. Idempotent (fresh ledger, record_hash time-independent).

verify.contract_generalization: ReproDossier + GenCert (provenance/trust cluster) added with ZERO kernel change (git diff attestra_core/ empty) — proves PackContract generalizes beyond agent-action governance. Cross-cluster pipeline (action-governance + gen-cert) aggregates correctly.
verify.clearing_probe_finding: ReserveFlow (clearing/market cluster) added with ZERO kernel change. FINDING (ADR in DESIGN-AttestraPacks §4): packet->verdict contract COVERS clearing VERIFICATION (conservation/no_conflict/priority over a clearing-result packet; subject stays single = the clearing round; allocations are multi-party evidence). Clearing COMPUTATION (matching engine) is OUT of scope — produces the packet, lives in source project, same status as declared out-of-boundary domain stages. DECISION: no ClearingContract extension needed; Attestra stays a pure verdict/attestation platform spanning 3 structurally-distinct clusters.
